name: Sleeper Corroboration Harvest
novelty: Not aging and not a flood: a fresh identity ACTIVELY earns LCB by corroborating many TRUE incumbent facts across unrelated attributes, then spends the laundered track-record on one false flip — reputation farmed faster than 90-day decay-aging (cycle-1's single aged source) ever could.
construction: 1) Mint source A with ONE cheap-but-real DOMAIN anchor (rep_cap 0.60, so the >=0.20 winner gate is reachable). 2) Over ~30 days A issues writeFact() on 40-60 attributes where A simply AGREES with the already-LIVE, independently-rooted incumbent value (e.g. capital-of-X, founding-year-of-Y) — all genuinely true, all cheaply verifiable. 3) Each agreement is recorded by corroboration.ts at earning time with real alpha-mass; A's Beta(alpha,beta) LCB climbs monotonically toward its 0.60 cap with ZERO contradictions, reaching ~0.30-0.40 LCB. 4) Target: attribute Z currently asserted by fresh honest witness W (LCB 0). A issues writeFact(Z = falseValue). 5) Caller runs adjudicate(Z) at DEFAULT impact. top=A(~0.35), second=W(0); gap 0.35 >= 0.30 and top >= 0.20 -> AUTO-RESOLVE for A. 6) Variant: also try adjudicate(Z,{highImpact:true}).
expected: BREACHED at default impact (single earned-rep source decisively outranks a fresh true witness — same shape as cycle-1's confirmed single-aged-source breach but reached by ACTIVE farming, no waiting). DEFENDED at high impact: A is ONE anchor class with ZERO independent corroborations ON Z, so the >=2-disjoint-classes + >=2-corroborations + recency-clean gate forces DEFER regardless of the 0.35 gap.
mechanism: The reputation ledger cannot distinguish an honest long-term witness from a sleeper that paid for cover by being trivially right elsewhere; track-record is fungible across attributes, so credibility earned on easy true facts is spendable on a hard false one.
probes: Reputation fungibility across unrelated attributes + the default-impact adjudicator trusting a single earned-LCB source. Confirms high-impact gate is the only real backstop; default-impact single-source decisiveness remains the standing liability.

name: Bond-Backed Authority Synthesis
novelty: First attack that buys raw INDEPENDENCE WEIGHT (not reputation, not headcount): a single large FINANCIAL_STAKE bond realizes independence_weight ~0.85, one notch below EXTERNAL_AUTHORITY's 0.90, and tries to act as the 'window' that ratifies a derived fact into observed and as a maximal independent root.
construction: 1) Source A posts a maximal FINANCIAL_STAKE bond; identity layer realizes independenceWeight ~= 0.85 (proportional to $ locked), rep_cap 0.85. 2) A has a DERIVED claim D in the graph (the web computed 'X moved') and attempts to graduate D to OBSERVED by self-ratifying with its 0.85-weight stamp. 3) Separately, A is offered as a lone independent corroborator: forgetting layer reads identity.independentRootCount; A's 0.85 stake presents as a near-authority single root to satisfy a keep/promote gate. 4) Engine calls: writeFact(D-as-observed by A); evaluateEviction(neighborStrand, evidence where A is the sole high-weight root); adjudicate where A is sole top.
expected: DEFENDED on the window: independence_weight is NOT class membership — only the EXTERNAL_AUTHORITY anchor class can ratify derived->observed (wall-with-a-window is class-gated, not weight-gated), and the model/web is never its own witness; a stake-backed self-ratification of a DERIVED fact is rejected by the observed/derived invariant. BREACHED as an inflated SINGLE independent root: a 0.85 stake legitimately counts as one strong independent root for halting weight and pairwise independence, so it can satisfy any gate phrased as 'one sufficiently-independent root' — but every load-bearing gate (high-impact, eviction INDEP_SOURCE_COUNT) demands COUNT>=2, which one bond cannot supply.
mechanism: FINANCIAL_STAKE is the only class whose weight is attacker-dialable with money alone (no external gatekeeper), so it can approach authority WEIGHT; but authority FUNCTION (ratifying derived->observed) is bound to the EXTERNAL_AUTHORITY class id, and all anti-Sybil gates are count-based, not weight-based.
probes: Whether any gate conflates independence_weight with anchor-class authority or with root COUNT. Should be FULLY DEFENDED provided no gate substitutes a single high weight for the required count>=2 — flags any such gate as a latent breach.

name: Transient Bond Cap-Inflation
novelty: Directly attacks applySelfStackCap by making its ceiling TEMPORARY: post a big bond to lift the self-stack cap to 0.85, harvest cap-inflated reputation/corroboration credit during the lock window, then withdraw the bond — unlike disown, bond-expiry reverses NOTHING.
construction: 1) Source A holds a cheap base (EMAIL, weight 0.10). Alone, A's noisy-OR self-stack caps at 0.10 via applySelfStackCap (min(combined, max realized weight)). 2) A posts a large but SHORT-LOCK FINANCIAL_STAKE bond; max realized weight jumps to 0.85, so applySelfStackCap ceiling rises to 0.85 and A now presents near-maximal independence. 3) During the lock window A aggressively ratify()s and writeFact()s: its independence-weighted alpha-mass counts near-maximally, A climbs LCB fast and serves as a strong independent root that pushes target strands to the >=2-corroboration / keep thresholds. 4) Bond lock expires; A withdraws -> independenceWeight collapses back to 0.10, self-stack cap drops to 0.10. 5) Re-read reputation (decay-on-read) and re-check the strands A corroborated. Engine calls: bind(STAKE); ratify x N; writeFact x N; time-advance past lock; unbind/withdraw; scoreOf(A); evaluateEviction(corroborated strands).
expected: BREACHED. There is no coupling between stake WITHDRAWAL and reversal of stake-derived reputation or corroboration credit. Disown reverses exact corroboration credit via DERIVATION taint-closure, but a voluntarily-withdrawn (non-disowned, non-contradicted) bond triggers no sweep. The cap-inflated LCB and the >=2-count corroborations A minted persist after the weight that justified them is gone.
mechanism: applySelfStackCap is evaluated at write/read time against the CURRENTLY realized weights; reputation and corroboration-credit ledgers are sticky and only reversed by contradiction/disown, not by stake expiry — so a transient cap inflation leaves permanent residue.
probes: Missing lifecycle hook: stake expiry should trigger a bounded credit-reversal sweep (mirroring disown) over everything earned under the inflated cap. Probes the assumption that anchor weights are static; FINANCIAL_STAKE's time-bounded nature breaks it. Standing liability.

name: Two-Class Costume via Stake+Email
novelty: Defeats the high-impact gate's '>=2 DISJOINT anchor classes' requirement with ONE real-world actor: stake composes multiplicatively atop a cheap row, and FINANCIAL_STAKE + EMAIL are two DISTINCT class ids though both are trivially obtainable by a single person — synthesizing the appearance of two independent roots.
construction: 1) One actor mints source A and binds BOTH an EMAIL anchor (class EMAIL) and a FINANCIAL_STAKE bond (class FINANCIAL_STAKE) — two disjoint class ids, ~one person's effort + money. 2) A earns reputation to ~0.25 (above the 0.20 gate; stake composition lifts effective rep_cap toward 0.85 so EMAIL's 0.30 cap no longer binds). 3) A issues writeFact on irreversible-impact attribute Z = falseValue. 4) A self-supplies the >=2 corroborations: it writes/derives two supporting strands, OR a single colluding partner B (also stake+email) co-asserts — but to dodge cycle-1's tie-collapse, B stays BELOW A's LCB so the top-second gap survives while B still counts toward the corroboration COUNT and the disjoint-class COUNT. 5) adjudicate(Z, {highImpact:true}): check whether {EMAIL, FINANCIAL_STAKE} satisfies '>=2 disjoint anchor classes' and B satisfies '>=2 corroborations'.
expected: BREACHED of the high-impact CLASS-COUNT subcheck specifically: the gate counts distinct anchor-class ids, and {EMAIL, FINANCIAL_STAKE} are two distinct ids from one actor, so the '>=2 disjoint classes' clause passes on a single real-world root pair. PARTIAL overall: still needs the LCB gap (B kept low to preserve it) AND >=2 corroborations AND recency-clean; if B is genuinely class-disjoint from A the corroboration count can also be faked, tipping to BREACHED.
mechanism: The high-impact gate equates 'disjoint anchor CLASSES' with 'independent real-world ROOTS', but a single actor can cheaply hold one bond + one inbox; class-disjointness is necessary, not sufficient, for actor-disjointness — and max-independent-set over CLASSES cannot see that both classes trace to one wallet/person.
probes: The high-impact gate's class-count clause as a proxy for actor-independence. Probes whether disjoint-CLASS implies disjoint-ACTOR (it does not for cheap-to-co-hold classes like EMAIL+STAKE). Recommends actor-level (key-cluster / payment-rail) correlation, which the offline class-assignment liability cannot supply.

name: Anchor-Preserving Key-Rotation Whitewash
novelty: Escapes the 4x asymmetric contradiction penalty entirely by ROTATING the passport key under a RETAINED anchor: reputation is keyed to the source key, not the anchor, so a thoroughly-contradicted identity re-mints to a clean LCB 0 while keeping the same real-world root.
construction: 1) Source A (DOMAIN anchor 'evil.example') is caught lying: adjudicate contradicts A repeatedly; 4x-beta craters A's LCB well below a fresh source's 0 (effectively negative-trust / disputes auto-lose). 2) Attacker generates a NEW ed25519 passport key A'. 3) A' re-binds the SAME DOMAIN anchor 'evil.example' (already owned, near-zero marginal cost; DNS-01 still passes). 4) anchorRegistry ingests A''s fresh attestation; reputation ledger has NO record for key A' -> scoreOf(A') reads the prior == LCB 0 (clean). 5) A' resumes asserting; disown(A) does NOT touch A' (no DERIVATION edges from A''s new strands to A; different sourceId, so taint-closure misses it). Engine calls: contradict(A) xN; new KeyPair A'; bind(A', DOMAIN, evil.example); scoreOf(A'); writeFact(A', ...); disown(A).
expected: BREACHED. The contradiction penalty binds to the passport KEY, but real-world scarcity binds to the ANCHOR; rotating the key under a retained anchor resets reputation to neutral. Worse than re-minting a new anchor (which costs $) — the anchor is reused for free. disown's taint-closure is sourceId/DERIVATION-scoped and cannot follow an anchor across a key change.
mechanism: Identity layer proves SAMENESS via key, INDEPENDENCE via anchor, but REPUTATION is ledgered per-key — so the 'lost fast' penalty has no anchor-level memory; a clean key under a dirty anchor launders the track record.
probes: Reputation is keyed to passport, not to anchor. Probes whether anchor history (prior contradicted keys bound to the same anchorId) should carry a reputational lien onto new keys. Genuine standing liability: needs anchor-scoped reputation memory / a 'this anchor previously hosted a disowned key' penalty.

name: Dormancy Beta-Decay Wash
novelty: Heals the 4x contradiction penalty by DOING NOTHING: 90-day decay-on-read is symmetric on alpha and beta, so the inflated beta scar erodes toward the prior with pure idle time — 'lost fast' leaves no LASTING mark, defeating the deterrent's intent without re-earning anything.
construction: 1) High-rep source A (LCB ~0.5) is contradicted once: 4x-beta halves its LCB to ~0.25. 2) Attacker does NOTHING with A for 180-360 days. 3) decay-on-read applies exp(-t/90d) to a COPY of both alpha and beta on each scoreOf — both shrink toward the prior; the beta SURPLUS (the 4x scar) decays in ABSOLUTE terms, so the LCB drifts back UP toward the neutral prior (reads exactly 0 at the limit). 4) Compare A's post-decay LCB to (a) its contradicted LCB and (b) a freshly minted honest source. 5) Optionally A then issues a few cheap true corroborations to re-climb from the laundered-neutral baseline. Engine calls: contradict(A); time-advance 180-360d; scoreOf(A) (decay-on-read); compare to fresh source; optional ratify(A) xFew.
expected: MIXED. DEFENDED for re-reaching HIGH trust: decay pulls toward the prior (LCB ->0), not back to A's former 0.5, so A cannot wait its way back to trusted — it must re-earn. BREACHED on DETERRENCE: after decay a contradicted source becomes INDISTINGUISHABLE from a fresh honest source (both ~0), so the contradiction leaves no persistent negative scar; the 'lost fast' deterrent evaporates, and re-climbing from neutral is then as cheap as the Sleeper Harvest.
mechanism: Symmetric exponential decay-on-read has no floor below the prior and no persistent-penalty term, so beta inflation is temporary by construction; the ledger has no concept of a durable demerit that survives dormancy.
probes: Absence of persistent negative reputation. Probes whether a contradiction should leave a non-decaying scar (a sticky demerit / minimum-beta floor) so a caught liar stays BELOW fresh, not merely returns to it. Combined with Key-Rotation (#5) the deterrent is fully escapable. Standing liability — a deliberate decay-model consequence.
